Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-21 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum-Ranker-Long Today's data shows two exceptional performers in the long-term momentum rankings. CVE (Crestwood Equity Partners) ranks #1 with a score of 93.0, displaying extraordinary returns of 128% over 1 year and 277% over 5 years, though its 10-year return of 104% lags significantly, suggesting it may be a cyclical or recently revived asset. REX (REX American Resources) matches CVE's 1-year return at 128% but extends much further back with a remarkable 457% 10-year return, indicating sustained long-term outperformance across multiple time horizons. Both securities pass all four momentum ratio thresholds and trade above all major moving averages, though CVE shows a concerning "thin" volatility profile and underperforms SPY by 155% over 10 years, while REX actually outperforms SPY by 197% over the same decade. CVE's strong correlation (0.74) with energy sector ETF XLE and negative correlation with consumer discretionary (XLY at -0.287) suggests its strength is tied to commodity cycles, whereas REX's more diversified performance across energy and precious metals benchmarks indicates broader secular tailwinds.
